creator: eugen@freebsd.org
text:
> Once it's there, it should (ideally) be checked and updated upon every port update so it stays accurate.

Minimal version requirement does not stale with time until the port builds. There is no need to check it unless a problem rises.

> Implicit (and even explicit) version requirements for dependencies are all over the place, but we don't record them because doing so does not scale.

This statement contradicts the Porter's Handbook as far I remember. In contrary, Ports framework has means to specify needed minimal dependency version. I do not want to state one must do that all the time but it helps port users and make a port more user friendly. And the whole goal of ports is to serve users, so more user friendliness should be encouraged, not suppressed.
